name: Potential Log4Shell Activity (Flows)
notes:
This rule triggers when potential Remote Code Execution related to Log4Shell Exploit (CVE-2021-44228) is observed. This could indicate an attacker bypassing it via techniques such as system environment variables, lower or upper lookup, invalid unicode characters with upper, system properties, html url encoding and notations.

The custom function LOG4J::DETECTED, accepts both string and integer values and returns true if they contain log4j strings.

Note: To view the events that would trigger this rule, use the search "Potential Log4Shell Detected (Flows)".
conditions_text: when the flow matches ("HTTP GET Request" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP GET Request", 40) == TRUE) OR ("HTTP Referer" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP Referer", 40) == TRUE) OR ("HTTP User-Agent" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP User-Agent", 40) == TRUE) OR ("HTTP Host" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP Host", 40) == TRUE) OR ("HTTP Server" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP Server", 40) == TRUE) OR ("HTTP Content-Type" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP Content-Type", 40) == TRUE) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential Log4Shell Activity (Flows) on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the flow matches ("HTTP GET Request" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP GET Request", 40) == TRUE) OR ("HTTP Referer" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP Referer", 40) == TRUE) OR ("HTTP User-Agent" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP User-Agent", 40) == TRUE) OR ("HTTP Host" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP Host", 40) == TRUE) OR ("HTTP Server" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP Server", 40) == TRUE) OR ("HTTP Content-Type" IS NOT NULL AND LOG4J::DETECTED("HTTP Content-Type", 40) == TRUE) AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: Detects when potential Remote Code Execution related to Log4Shell Exploit (CVE-2021-44228) is observed. This could indicate an attacker bypassing it via techniques such as system environment variables, lower or upper lookup, invalid unicode characters with upper, system properties, html url encoding and notations.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
